e2e.summary: 12 passed / 6 failed across 6 viewports x 3 browsers (chromium, firefox, webkit). Failures isolated to chromium only due to SVG <circle> cx/cy parse errors (empty/NaN coordinates).

recommendations: Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers ~11KB
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers (saves ~150ms)

recommendations.action: Mobile path is shipping the same JS as desktop (R3F + three + drei + postprocessing). Move the dynamic `import('./_Scene')` INSIDE the `!isMobile && !isLowEnd` branch so the Scene chunk is never even prefetched on mobile. Currently next/dynamic is hoisted at module scope. Use a useEffect-driven import OR a conditional dynamic component. Expected mobile-bundle drop: ~150KB.

Chromium throws repeated `<circle> attribute cx/cy: Unexpected end of attribute. Expected length, "".` errors — failing all 6 chromium E2E tests. Find SVG <circle> elements where cx/cy values can be empty/undefined/NaN during initial render. Likely OrbitalBackdrop or PingRings. Default coordinates to 0 when unset; never let GSAP write empty strings. grep `cx=` and `cy=` for templates that use undefined refs.

Lighthouse mobile recommends 'reduce unused JavaScript ~154KB'. Check next.config.mjs and add `experimental: { optimizePackageImports: ['gsap', '@react-three/drei', '@react-three/fiber', 'three'] }` so Next prunes unused subpath exports. Also: explicitly import only the drei pieces actually used (e.g., `import { Float } from '@react-three/drei/web/Float'` rather than top-level).

Lighthouse mobile TBT 1572ms is a major blocker. The HeroWarrior + DevStation paths even when gated by `tier==='high'` may still execute their imports during initial parse. Wrap the entire HeroWarrior + DevStation modules behind `dynamic(() => import(), { ssr: false })` from the section component, so they're code-split out of the main route bundle.

Visual regression flagged the contact-section title wrapping awkwardly ('ING // THE_CO RE') at 375px and partially obscured by section badge '06' at 768px. Reduce title font size with clamp + adjust the section number badge z-index/position so it doesn't overlap. Verify with re-screenshot.

Visual regression overall score 45 is a FALSE NEGATIVE — caused by build corruption when 4 parallel agents ran `npm run start` against the same .next/ directory. Before the next iteration, do a clean `rm -rf .next && npm run build` and re-run visual regression in isolation (no parallel server starts). The actual layout is sound based on chromium-vs-firefox comparison.

Add Next.js compiler.removeConsole for production: `compiler: { removeConsole: { exclude: ['error', 'warn'] } }`. And add `swcMinify: true` (default in Next 14 but explicit). Also enable `productionBrowserSourceMaps: false` (default but verify) — sourcemaps in production add ~30% to served bundle.

score.summary: Iteration 1 of convergence loop: desktop perf 88 (target 90, -2), mobile perf 66 (target 75, -9), E2E 12/18 (chromium SVG NaN errors), visual 45 (build corruption false-negative). Primary blocker is mobile bundle size — Three.js stack still ships even on mobile fallback path. Secondary: chromium SVG cx/cy NaN guard, code-split HeroWarrior + DevStation, fix contact title wrap, re-run visual after clean build.

all other sections render as unstyled HTML fallback (no CSS, no canvas, no theme) (major)

no HUD frame, no orange/dark theme applied (major)

about/skills/projects/experience/contact all show unstyled HTML fallback (major)
no CSS or canvas/svg core loaded (major)
complete loss of design system at desktop breakpoint (major)
hero renders blank white canvas with only grid lines - main visual missing (major)
all sections render as unstyled HTML fallback (major)
no CSS or canvas/svg core loaded (major)
complete loss of design system at large desktop breakpoint (major)
contact section title 'OPENING // THE_CORE' wraps awkwardly producing 'ING // THE_CO RE' (major text break)
contact section 'OPEN' prefix appears clipped above viewport when scrolled to anchor
contact title still wraps as 'ING // THE_COR E' due to large font on narrow column (major)
skills HUD bottom: 'LAT 37.77°N / LON -122.42°W' overlaps 'OPENAI API' text row (minor)
contact title 'OPENING // THE_CORE' partially obscured by '06' badge overlapping 'OPEN' (major)
experience section second card placeholder shows 'CORE // ONLINE' overlap with HUD frame (minor)

e2e.summary: All 18 responsive E2E tests passed across chromium, firefox, and webkit (6 viewports each: iphone-se, iphone-11, ipad, ipad-land, laptop, desktop). No horizontal overflow, no fatal console errors, hero heading visible across all viewports. Run completed in ~35.6s against production server on port 3200.

recommendations: Reduce unused JavaScript (~110ms savings on desktop)
Add valid source maps for large first-party JavaScript (currently missing)
Reduce total byte weight of network payloads (score 0.5)
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers (score 0.5)
Eliminate render-blocking resources (~45ms savings on mobile)
Optimize Largest Contentful Paint element (LCP 2.77s on mobile)
Add valid source maps and reduce total byte weight / legacy JS

recommendations.action: On mobile (375/414) the SVG core fallback orb overlaps the hero 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' role badge. The fallback is full-viewport positioned. Make the orb's SVG smaller on small viewports (e.g., scale down with viewBox + max-w / smaller fixed center), and offset it vertically to the top half so the role-badge text below it has clear space. Suggested: limit orb visual to the top 55vh on viewports <= 480px and reduce orb radius proportionally.

The 'CORE // ONLINE' badge + orbiting orange dot is rendering as part of the SVG fallback or HUD overlay and is fixed to the viewport, causing it to bleed visually into projects/career/about sections during scroll. Wrap that badge in a hero-only ScrollTrigger that hides it when hero is scrolled past, OR position it inside the hero section element instead of fixed.

Contact title 'PING // THE_CORE' (or 'OPENING // THE_CORE') still wraps awkwardly to 'ING // THE_CORE' at 375px. The Phase 5 fix used keep-all + clamp(2rem,...) but the visual at 375px shows breakage still. Solution: split the title into two lines deliberately ('PING //' on line 1, 'THE_CORE' on line 2) on viewports <= 480px using a `<br className="sm:hidden">` or grid layout. Also reduce font size further on smallest viewport (clamp(1.5rem,...)).

On mobile (375/414) the projects section shows the SVG orb dominating and the project card content is not visible in the fold. The card is below the orb in the stack. Either: (a) reduce orb size to ~30vh on mobile so card stays in viewport, OR (b) hide the orb entirely on mobile in projects (it's already in hero — duplication is visual noise). Pick whichever fits the design language better.

Mobile hero role badge 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' is visually overlapped by the orb. Add a dark gradient/glass background to the role badge so it remains readable on top of any background, OR position the orb behind a translucent backdrop layer that slightly fades the orb where text overlaps.

score.summary: Iteration 2 RESULTS: desktop perf 99 (target 90, +9), mobile perf 95 (target 75, +20), E2E 18/18 (3 browsers x 6 viewports), visual 85. Performance and E2E targets are massively exceeded. Visual is at 85 but the mobile sub-scores (70 at 375px, 74 at 414px) show real layout issues that the user would consider 'not 100% solid'. One more refine pass to fix mobile orb overlap, persistent CORE badge bleed, and contact title wrap before declaring final convergence.

Hero orb canvas overlaps the 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' role badge text

Contact heading wraps awkwardly to 'ING // THE_CORE'
Projects section shows large orb but project card content not visible at this viewport
Hero orb visual overlaps 'FRONTEND DEVELOP' role badge text
Projects section: orb dominates and project card not visible in fold
Hero role badge slightly overlapped by orb visual

recommendations.action: Minor: at 1024x768 (tablet-landscape) the 'CORE // ONLINE' label still bleeds slightly into the LinkedIn link area at the bottom of contact. Bump the @media query in SvgCoreFallback.tsx from `(max-width: 640px)` to `(max-width: 1024px)` to also hide the badge at tablet-landscape. NON-BLOCKING for convergence.
Mobile orb fade currently goes from 1.0 to 0.15 with 0.85 multiplier. Consider increasing fade depth to 0.95 multiplier so background is even cleaner on small viewports. NON-BLOCKING.

score.summary: CONVERGED. Desktop LH 99 (target 90, +9). Mobile LH 97 (target 75, +22). E2E 18/18 across chromium/firefox/webkit at 6 viewports. Visual 88/100 (target 80, +8) with mobile sub-scores 80/82, tablet 86-92, desktop 94-95. Critical path issues resolved: dual R3F Canvas removed, Three.js code-split off mobile bundle, GLTF material clones, Lenis disabled on iOS+Firefox, 17 GSAP plugins reduced to 4, splitChars wraps words atomically, contact heading bug fixed, mobile orb fade implemented. Remaining items are minor polish that does not block convergence.

instructions: Write artifacts/perf-responsive-report.md with: status (converged or not), final Lighthouse desktop+mobile scores, E2E pass rate per browser+viewport, list of optimizations applied per phase, remaining caveats, recommended manual checks (real device test list — iPhone SE, iPad, Pixel low-end, mid-range Windows laptop).
Return JSON { reportPath, historyPath, verdict, summary }
